page: Amazon Web Services m5.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It is equipped with an Intel Xeon 8259CL x86_64 processor featuring 48 vCPUs, which correspond to 24 physical cores with Hyper-Threading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz and includes 35 MB of L3 cache. The instance is configured with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the m5.12xlarge delivers mid-tier performance across compute and memory subsystems. Its stress-ng single-core score of 1383.8 and multi-core score of 40455.21 fall within the average category. Memory bandwidth testing shows a read-only cached small block speed of 2252.55 GB/sec, which drops to 108.48 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. The instance demonstrates steady performance in data processing tasks, achieving a multi-threaded bzip3 decompression speed of 63.99 GB/sec and a static web serving throughput of 27.06 GB/sec.

The lack of local NVMe storage and GPU accelerators represents a clear tradeoff, making this instance less suitable for high-IOPS database caching or intensive deep learning tasks. While it handles prompt processing for small 135M LLM models at 1429.79 tokens/sec, performance drops significantly on larger models, with 70B model prompt processing rated as weak at 5.77 tokens/sec. The m5.12xlarge is best utilized for general-purpose workloads, including application servers, software compilation, and standard database deployments.
description: Amazon Web Services m5.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL x86_64 processor with 48 vCPUs and 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Lacking local storage and GPUs, it relies on network-attached storage and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. Benchmark results place its compute and memory performance consistently in the average tier, with a passmark CPU score of 34538.76 and a multi-core Geekbench score of 10676.0. It exhibits average performance across database operations, web serving, and small-to-medium LLM inference, but shows weak performance on larger 70B models. This instance is suitable for balanced workloads such as application servers, web serving, and standard database deployments.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 48 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 3.1 GHz processor, delivering balanced compute and memory performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 48 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 12 Gbps baseline network speed.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual server featuring forty-eight vCPUs and DDR4 memory for enterprise applications and web serving.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 48 vCPUs and 192 GB DDR4 RAM
Nitro hypervisor architecture with 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Average performance across CPU, memory, and database benchmarks
Suited for web serving, application servers, and general-purpose workloads
